  "text": "The TiDB privilege and RBAC enforcement system in v8.5.0 is structured across two main packages: pkg/privilege/ and pkg/planner/.\n\n## PrivilegeManager Interface\n\n`pkg/privilege/privilege.go` defines the `Manager` interface — the central contract for all privilege operations. Key methods include `RequestVerification(activeRole, db, table, column, priv)` for static privilege checks, `RequestDynamicVerification(activeRoles, privName, grantable)` for dynamic privileges (BACKUP_ADMIN, ROLE_ADMIN, etc.), `ConnectionVerification` for login auth, `ShowGrants`, `ActiveRoles`, `FindEdge`, `GetDefaultRoles`, and `GetAllRoles`. `GetPrivilegeManager` retrieves the bound Manager from the session context; `BindPrivilegeManager` sets it.\n\n## UserPrivileges (Manager implementation)\n\n`pkg/privilege/privileges/privileges.go` contains `UserPrivileges`, the concrete implementation of `privilege.Manager`. Its `RequestVerification` method: (1) handles SkipWithGrant bypass; (2) enforces SEM (Security Enhanced Mode) hard rules via `RequestDynamicVerification`; (3) invokes extension access-check hooks; (4) delegates to `MySQLPrivilege.RequestVerification` from the cache; (5) optionally calls an auth-plugin-specific verification function. `RequestDynamicVerification` similarly delegates to `MySQLPrivilege.RequestDynamicVerification`, which falls back to SUPER privilege for compatibility.\n\n## MySQLPrivilege Cache\n\n`pkg/privilege/privileges/cache.go` is the largest and most critical file. It defines:\n- `MySQLPrivilege` struct: the in-memory cache of all MySQL privilege tables (mysql.user, mysql.db, mysql.tables_priv, mysql.columns_priv, mysql.global_priv, mysql.global_grants, mysql.role_edges, mysql.default_roles). It is populated by `LoadAll` which issues SQL queries defined as constants (sqlLoadUserTable, sqlLoadDBTable, sqlLoadTablePrivTable, etc.).\n- `Handle` struct (line 1818): wraps a `*MySQLPrivilege` and provides `Get()` to retrieve the current snapshot and `Update()` to reload.\n- `roleGraphEdgesTable` (line 248): per-user map of role edges loaded from mysql.role_edges.\n- `roleGraph map[string]roleGraphEdgesTable` (line 275): the adjacency list of the role inheritance graph.\n- `LoadRoleGraph` (line 541): populates `roleGraph` from mysql.role_edges.\n- `FindRole` (line 340): checks if a direct edge exists between a user and role.\n- `FindAllRole` (line 315): BFS traversal of `roleGraph` to expand all transitively reachable roles from a set of active roles.\n- `FindAllUserEffectiveRoles` (line 304): filters `activeRoles` against granted roles for the user, then calls `FindAllRole` to do full BFS expansion — this is the core role graph resolution used before every privilege check.\n- `RequestVerification` (line 1333): calls `FindAllUserEffectiveRoles` to get the full role list, then checks user-level, db-level, table-level, and column-level privileges in order, OR-ing the privilege bitmask across all roles.\n- `RequestDynamicVerification` (line 1311): calls `HasExplicitlyGrantedDynamicPrivilege`, then falls back to SUPER if SEM permits.\n- `HasExplicitlyGrantedDynamicPrivilege` (line 1287): checks mysql.global_grants for the dynamic privilege, looping over all effective roles.\n\n## SQL Statement → Required Privileges Mapping\n\n`pkg/planner/core/planbuilder.go` defines the `visitInfo` struct (line 81) — the data structure recording what privilege is required for which db/table/column. Functions `appendVisitInfo` and `appendDynamicVisitInfo` (defined in `pkg/planner/core/logical_plan_builder.go`) populate the builder's `visitInfo` slice as each SQL clause is processed. `planbuilder.go` contains the main `Build` method dispatch and handles DDL statements (CREATE/DROP/ALTER TABLE, GRANT, REVOKE, SHOW, ADMIN, SET, KILL, etc.), mapping each to specific `mysql.XxxPriv` constants or dynamic privilege names. For example: SHOW CREATE TABLE → ShowViewPriv; SHOW PROCESSLIST → ProcessPriv; SET GLOBAL → SYSTEM_VARIABLES_ADMIN dynamic; KILL → CONNECTION_ADMIN dynamic; GRANT/REVOKE → GrantPriv + SuperPriv or dynamic.\n\n`pkg/planner/core/logical_plan_builder.go` handles DML: SELECT → SelectPriv, INSERT → InsertPriv, UPDATE → UpdatePriv, DELETE → DeletePriv. It also checks view definer privileges via `RequestVerificationWithUser` (line 5016) to enforce SQL SECURITY DEFINER semantics.\n\n## CheckPrivilege and Its Callers\n\n`pkg/planner/core/optimizer.go` defines `CheckPrivilege(activeRoles, pm, vs []visitInfo)` (line 126) — the function that iterates the visitInfo slice and calls `pm.RequestDynamicVerification` for ExtendedPriv entries or `pm.RequestVerification` for all others. `VisitInfo4PrivCheck` (line 155) post-processes visitInfo to handle local temporary table privilege semantics.\n\nCallers:\n- `pkg/planner/optimize.go` (line 501): calls `GetPrivilegeManager` + `VisitInfo4PrivCheck` + `CheckPrivilege` after the logical plan is built.\n- `pkg/planner/core/plan_cache.go` `checkPreparedPriv` (line 327): re-checks privileges on each execution of a prepared statement.\n- `pkg/planner/core/preprocess.go` (line 513): calls `RequestVerification` directly during AST pre-processing for database visibility.\n- `pkg/planner/core/point_get_plan.go` (line 1499): calls `RequestVerification` directly for PointGet fast path.\n- `pkg/planner/core/expression_codec_fn.go` (line 91): calls `checker.RequestVerification` via the `expropt.PrivilegeChecker` interface for the TIDB_DECODE_TABLE_KEY built-in function.\n\n## PrivilegeChecker Interface\n\n`pkg/expression/expropt/priv.go` defines the `PrivilegeChecker` interface (a narrower interface with just `RequestVerification`) used by built-in expression functions that need privilege checks without a full session context dependency.\n\n## Auth / Connection Layer\n\n`pkg/privilege/conn/conn.go` defines `AuthConn`, the interface used by auth plugins (LDAP SASL, extension plugins) to exchange additional packets during authentication.\n\n`pkg/privilege/privileges/ldap/` implements LDAP authentication backends (SASL and Simple), invoked from `UserPrivileges.ConnectionVerification`.\n\n`pkg/privilege/privileges/tidb_auth_token.go` and `pkg/privilege/privileges/errors.go` provide JWT token auth support and privilege-related error definitions respectively."

# TiDB SQL Privilege Check and RBAC Enforcement Audit

## Your Task

Audit the privilege and RBAC enforcement in pingcap/tidb. Find all Go source files under pkg/privilege/ and pkg/planner/ that implement privilege checking for SQL statements. Identify: the PrivilegeManager interface, the MySQLPrivilege cache, the RequestVerification method and its callers, the role graph resolution logic, and any files that map SQL statement types to required privileges. For each file report the path and its role in the privilege check flow.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the compliance domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/tidb--v8.5.0.

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?

Now let me look at the conn package and check if there are any other relevant files:
Now I have a comprehensive view of all the relevant files. Let me compile the findings into the answer:
Now I have all the information needed to write the comprehensive answer:

The answer has been written to `/workspace/answer.json`. Here's a summary of the findings:

## Key Files in the TiDB Privilege System

### `pkg/privilege/` — Core privilege infrastructure

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `privilege.go` | Defines the `Manager` interface (the PrivilegeManager contract with `RequestVerification`, `RequestDynamicVerification`, `ConnectionVerification`, etc.) |
| `privileges/privileges.go` | `UserPrivileges` — concrete `Manager` implementation; wraps `MySQLPrivilege` cache, applies SEM rules, dispatches to auth plugins |
| `privileges/cache.go` | `MySQLPrivilege` struct — in-memory cache of all MySQL privilege tables; contains `RequestVerification`, `RequestDynamicVerification`, `roleGraph` + `FindAllRole`/`FindAllUserEffectiveRoles` for RBAC role graph resolution, and `Handle` for cache lifecycle |
| `privileges/errors.go` | Privilege error definitions |
| `privileges/tidb_auth_token.go` | JWT/TiDB auth token validation |
| `conn/conn.go` | `AuthConn` interface for auth plugin packet exchange |
| `privileges/ldap/` | LDAP SASL and Simple auth backends |

### `pkg/planner/` — SQL → privilege mapping and enforcement

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `core/planbuilder.go` | Defines `visitInfo` struct; maps DDL/admin SQL statement types to required privileges via `appendVisitInfo`/`appendDynamicVisitInfo` |
| `core/logical_plan_builder.go` | DML privilege mapping (SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE); defines `appendVisitInfo` and `appendDynamicVisitInfo` helpers; view definer privilege check |
| `core/optimizer.go` | `CheckPrivilege` — main enforcement function iterating visitInfo and calling `pm.RequestVerification`; `VisitInfo4PrivCheck` for temp table handling |
| `optimize.go` | Calls `CheckPrivilege` after plan build |
| `core/plan_cache.go` | Re-checks privileges on prepared statement execution |
| `core/preprocess.go` | Direct `RequestVerification` call during AST preprocessing |
| `core/point_get_plan.go` | Direct `RequestVerification` for PointGet fast path |
| `core/expression_codec_fn.go` | `RequestVerification` via `PrivilegeChecker` for codec built-ins |

### `pkg/expression/expropt/priv.go`
Defines `PrivilegeChecker` interface — a lightweight interface used by built-in functions needing privilege checks.

   125→// CheckPrivilege checks the privilege for a user.
   126→func CheckPrivilege(activeRoles []*auth.RoleIdentity, pm privilege.Manager, vs []visitInfo) error {
   127→	for _, v := range vs {
   128→		if v.privilege == mysql.ExtendedPriv {
   129→			hasPriv := false
   130→			for _, priv := range v.dynamicPrivs {
   131→				hasPriv = hasPriv || pm.RequestDynamicVerification(activeRoles, priv, v.dynamicWithGrant)
   132→				if hasPriv {
   133→					break
   134→				}
   135→			}
   136→			if !hasPriv {
   137→				if v.err == nil {
   138→					return plannererrors.ErrPrivilegeCheckFail.GenWithStackByArgs(v.dynamicPrivs)
   139→				}
   140→				return v.err
   141→			}
   142→		} else if !pm.RequestVerification(activeRoles, v.db, v.table, v.column, v.privilege) {
   143→			if v.err == nil {
   144→				return plannererrors.ErrPrivilegeCheckFail.GenWithStackByArgs(v.privilege.String())
   145→			}
   146→			return v.err
   147→		}
   148→	}
   149→	return nil
   150→}

   152→// VisitInfo4PrivCheck generates privilege check infos because privilege check of local temporary tables is different
   153→// with normal tables. `CREATE` statement needs `CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE` privilege from the database, and subsequent
   154→// statements do not need any privileges.
   155→func VisitInfo4PrivCheck(ctx context.Context, is infoschema.InfoSchema, node ast.Node, vs []visitInfo) (privVisitInfo []visitInfo) {
   156→	if node == nil {
   157→		return vs
   158→	}
   159→
   160→	switch stmt := node.(type) {
   161→	case *ast.CreateTableStmt:
   162→		privVisitInfo = make([]visitInfo, 0, len(vs))
   163→		for _, v := range vs {
   164→			if v.privilege == mysql.CreatePriv {
   165→				if stmt.TemporaryKeyword == ast.TemporaryLocal {
   166→					// `CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE` privilege is required from the database, not the table.
   167→					newVisitInfo := v
   168→					newVisitInfo.privilege = mysql.CreateTMPTablePriv
   169→					newVisitInfo.table = ""
   170→					privVisitInfo = append(privVisitInfo, newVisitInfo)
   171→				} else {
   172→					// If both the normal table and temporary table already exist, we need to check the privilege.
   173→					privVisitInfo = append(privVisitInfo, v)
   174→				}
   175→			} else {
   176→				// `CREATE TABLE LIKE tmp` or `CREATE TABLE FROM SELECT tmp` in the future.
   177→				if needCheckTmpTablePriv(ctx, is, v) {
   178→					privVisitInfo = append(privVisitInfo, v)
   179→				}
   180→			}
   181→		}

   183→		// Dropping a local temporary table doesn't need any privileges.

   185→			privVisitInfo = vs
   186→		} else {
   187→			privVisitInfo = make([]visitInfo, 0, len(vs))
   188→			if stmt.TemporaryKeyword != ast.TemporaryLocal {
   189→				for _, v := range vs {

   192→					}

   197→		// Some statements ignore local temporary tables, so they should check the privileges on normal tables.
   198→		privVisitInfo = vs

   200→		privVisitInfo = make([]visitInfo, 0, len(vs))

  "text": "The TiDB privilege and RBAC enforcement system in v8.5.0 is structured across two main packages: pkg/privilege/ and pkg/planner/.\n\n## PrivilegeManager Interface\n\n`pkg/privilege/privilege.go` defines the `Manager` interface — the central contract for all privilege operations. Key methods include `RequestVerification(activeRole, db, table, column, priv)` for static privilege checks, `RequestDynamicVerification(activeRoles, privName, grantable)` for dynamic privileges (BACKUP_ADMIN, ROLE_ADMIN, etc.), `ConnectionVerification` for login auth, `ShowGrants`, `ActiveRoles`, `FindEdge`, `GetDefaultRoles`, and `GetAllRoles`. `GetPrivilegeManager` retrieves the bound Manager from the session context; `BindPrivilegeManager` sets it.\n\n## UserPrivileges (Manager implementation)\n\n`pkg/privilege/privileges/privileges.go` contains `UserPrivileges`, the concrete implementation of `privilege.Manager`. Its `RequestVerification` method: (1) handles SkipWithGrant bypass; (2) enforces SEM (Security Enhanced Mode) hard rules via `RequestDynamicVerification`; (3) invokes extension access-check hooks; (4) delegates to `MySQLPrivilege.RequestVerification` from the cache; (5) optionally calls an auth-plugin-specific verification function. `RequestDynamicVerification` similarly delegates to `MySQLPrivilege.RequestDynamicVerification`, which falls back to SUPER privilege for compatibility.\n\n## MySQLPrivilege Cache\n\n`pkg/privilege/privileges/cache.go` is the largest and most critical file. It defines:\n- `MySQLPrivilege` struct: the in-memory cache of all MySQL privilege tables (mysql.user, mysql.db, mysql.tables_priv, mysql.columns_priv, mysql.global_priv, mysql.global_grants, mysql.role_edges, mysql.default_roles). It is populated by `LoadAll` which issues SQL queries defined as constants (sqlLoadUserTable, sqlLoadDBTable, sqlLoadTablePrivTable, etc.).\n- `Handle` struct (line 1818): wraps a `*MySQLPrivilege` and provides `Get()` to retrieve the current snapshot and `Update()` to reload.\n- `roleGraphEdgesTable` (line 248): per-user map of role edges loaded from mysql.role_edges.\n- `roleGraph map[string]roleGraphEdgesTable` (line 275): the adjacency list of the role inheritance graph.\n- `LoadRoleGraph` (line 541): populates `roleGraph` from mysql.role_edges.\n- `FindRole` (line 340): checks if a direct edge exists between a user and role.\n- `FindAllRole` (line 315): BFS traversal of `roleGraph` to expand all transitively reachable roles from a set of active roles.\n- `FindAllUserEffectiveRoles` (line 304): filters `activeRoles` against granted roles for the user, then calls `FindAllRole` to do full BFS expansion — this is the core role graph resolution used before every privilege check.\n- `RequestVerification` (line 1333): calls `FindAllUserEffectiveRoles` to get the full role list, then checks user-level, db-level, table-level, and column-level privileges in order, OR-ing the privilege bitmask across all roles.\n- `RequestDynamicVerification` (line 1311): calls `HasExplicitlyGrantedDynamicPrivilege`, then falls back to SUPER if SEM permits.\n- `HasExplicitlyGrantedDynamicPrivilege` (line 1287): checks mysql.global_grants for the dynamic privilege, looping over all effective roles.\n\n## SQL Statement → Required Privileges Mapping\n\n`pkg/planner/core/planbuilder.go` defines the `visitInfo` struct (line 81) — the data structure recording what privilege is required for which db/table/column. Functions `appendVisitInfo` and `appendDynamicVisitInfo` (defined in `pkg/planner/core/logical_plan_builder.go`) populate the builder's `visitInfo` slice as each SQL clause is processed. `planbuilder.go` contains the main `Build` method dispatch and handles DDL statements (CREATE/DROP/ALTER TABLE, GRANT, REVOKE, SHOW, ADMIN, SET, KILL, etc.), mapping each to specific `mysql.XxxPriv` constants or dynamic privilege names. For example: SHOW CREATE TABLE → ShowViewPriv; SHOW PROCESSLIST → ProcessPriv; SET GLOBAL → SYSTEM_VARIABLES_ADMIN dynamic; KILL → CONNECTION_ADMIN dynamic; GRANT/REVOKE → GrantPriv + SuperPriv or dynamic.\n\n`pkg/planner/core/logical_plan_builder.go` handles DML: SELECT → SelectPriv, INSERT → InsertPriv, UPDATE → UpdatePriv, DELETE → DeletePriv. It also checks view definer privileges via `RequestVerificationWithUser` (line 5016) to enforce SQL SECURITY DEFINER semantics.\n\n## CheckPrivilege and Its Callers\n\n`pkg/planner/core/optimizer.go` defines `CheckPrivilege(activeRoles, pm, vs []visitInfo)` (line 126) — the function that iterates the visitInfo slice and calls `pm.RequestDynamicVerification` for ExtendedPriv entries or `pm.RequestVerification` for all others. `VisitInfo4PrivCheck` (line 155) post-processes visitInfo to handle local temporary table privilege semantics.\n\nCallers:\n- `pkg/planner/optimize.go` (line 501): calls `GetPrivilegeManager` + `VisitInfo4PrivCheck` + `CheckPrivilege` after the logical plan is built.\n- `pkg/planner/core/plan_cache.go` `checkPreparedPriv` (line 327): re-checks privileges on each execution of a prepared statement.\n- `pkg/planner/core/preprocess.go` (line 513): calls `RequestVerification` directly during AST pre-processing for database visibility.\n- `pkg/planner/core/point_get_plan.go` (line 1499): calls `RequestVerification` directly for PointGet fast path.\n- `pkg/planner/core/expression_codec_fn.go` (line 91): calls `checker.RequestVerification` via the `expropt.PrivilegeChecker` interface for the TIDB_DECODE_TABLE_KEY built-in function.\n\n## PrivilegeChecker Interface\n\n`pkg/expression/expropt/priv.go` defines the `PrivilegeChecker` interface (a narrower interface with just `RequestVerification`) used by built-in expression functions that need privilege checks without a full session context dependency.\n\n## Auth / Connection Layer\n\n`pkg/privilege/conn/conn.go` defines `AuthConn`, the interface used by auth plugins (LDAP SASL, extension plugins) to exchange additional packets during authentication.\n\n`pkg/privilege/privileges/ldap/` implements LDAP authentication backends (SASL and Simple), invoked from `UserPrivileges.ConnectionVerification`.\n\n`pkg/privilege/privileges/tidb_auth_token.go` and `pkg/privilege/privileges/errors.go` provide JWT token auth support and privilege-related error definitions respectively."
